text: fix login
run tests
review this
Please fix the login button staying disabled after failed auth, keep the diff small, run tests, and summarize files changed.
Can you inspect the repo and find where storage is handled? I do not need code changes, just explain the relevant files.
Add a CSV export button to src/components/ReportTable.tsx, match existing style, run the tests, and tell me what changed.
The checkout page flashes a blank screen after applying a coupon. Fix it with the smallest safe patch and do not refactor unrelated code.
Update README.md for the new --json flag. Keep wording concise and include one powershell example.
Refactor duplicated validation logic in tokensquash/codec.py without adding dependencies, run lint, and give me a short summary.
Find the API retry code and explain how it behaves when the server returns 429.
Please build a tiny repro test for the cache expiry bug, keep the public API backwards compatible, and run the unit tests.
Audit the billing webhook handler for obvious edge cases. Do not change code yet; return risks and the files you inspected.
Add type hints to the public helper functions in src/utils/date.ts, run typecheck, and avoid changing runtime behavior.
The navbar overlaps content on mobile. Fix it, match existing CSS patterns, and tell me which files changed.
plz check why npm test is failing and make the smallest fix you can
Make the settings modal close on Escape, add coverage if there is an obvious test place, run tests, summarize.
Where is the sqlite connection created? I want the answer with paths, not a patch.
Fix failing lint in src/api/client.ts. No new dependencies. Return the commands you ran.
Please implement dark mode persistence using localStorage, keep the UI style consistent, run tests and typecheck.
The docs mention repomori but this repo is TokenSquash now. Update the docs and keep the wording short.
Can you compare the current benchmark output with the old one and tell me if savings improved?
Investigate why the CLI exits 0 when the benchmark misses the target. Fix if confirmed, add a test, run tests.
Add a --out option to bench so it can write the JSON report to disk, and document it in the README.
Review the parser for injection or malformed input problems. Return risks only; do not patch unless it is clearly broken.
The wire format is a bit too verbose. Try to reduce it, but preserve decode round-trip tests.
Fix Windows path handling for C:\Users\me\repo\src\app.py in the path extractor and run tests.
Add JSONL corpus validation with useful line numbers. Keep it dependency free and summarize files changed.
Please look at examples/coding-prompts.jsonl and add more realistic prompts, including short ones that should pass through.
Can the benchmark report raw wire savings as well as adaptive savings? Add that and update tests.
I need a GitHub Actions job that installs the tokenizer extra and runs tiktoken:cl100k_base on the messy corpus.
Explain the difference between heuristic, char4, and tiktoken counters in plain language.
Fix the failing test_benchmark_reports_savings without relaxing the assertion too much.
Add support for return=risks in the compact protocol and make sure decode says risks or caveats.
Review README.md for claims that sound too strong. Tone it down and keep the project honest.
Find every place where target_savings_pct is used and explain the pass/fail behavior.
Make parse_wire accept unquoted query words after the op, then add a regression test.
Please build a benchmark artifact under artifacts/benchmarks and include both markdown and json output.
Run the sample benchmark using the exact tokenizer if tiktoken is installed; otherwise tell me what is missing.
Fix docs typo in the install section, no code changes needed.
Review the whole repo and suggest the next three experiments, with risks and expected token impact.
Add a compact reply schema for agent results: changed files, tests run, summary, risks.
Implement decode-reply for the compact reply schema, add tests, and document examples.
The CLI should print errors to stderr and return 2 on invalid input. Check that behavior and add coverage.
Can you make the benchmark rows include the selected operation, constraints, verification, and returns?
Fix the operation detector so 'do not refactor' does not classify the task as refactor.
Add support for 'no deps' and 'avoid dependencies' phrases in the constraint extractor.
Please inspect the compact query builder. It leaves filler words in some rows; trim them without losing meaning.
Run compileall over the package and tests, then summarize whether anything failed.
Where should RepoMori integrate with TokenSquash? Give me a small architecture proposal, no code.
Make the README first paragraph clearer for someone who has never heard of token compression.
Add a license placeholder or explain that licensing is undecided. Do not invent legal text.
Review pyproject.toml metadata and add missing project urls if appropriate.
The exact tokenizer benchmark is slower in CI. Cache pip if easy, but keep the workflow simple.
Add tests for JSONL rows using 'prompt' instead of 'text' and for bad rows missing both keys.
Fix path extraction for src/App.test.tsx and package-lock.json, then run tests.
Can you add a corpus stats command that counts prompts, average length, and shortest/longest rows?
The adaptive benchmark should count pass-through rows separately. Add that metric and docs.
Please verify the GitHub Actions run on the latest push and tell me if it is green.
Update docs to say the current corpus is synthetic and not proof of production savings.
Try a benchmark with --no-adaptive and compare against adaptive mode. Return the difference.
Fix any formatting issues you see in metrics.py, keep the change small, run tests.
Add a future work section for real-world prompt collection, privacy filtering, and model tokenizer comparisons.
Please create a minimal MCP tool design for encode and bench. No implementation yet.
Add a failing test first for 'smallest possible patch' mapping to small_diff, then make it pass.
The query phrase 'what changed?' should probably return op=query, not task. Check and fix if needed.
Can you make the benchmark markdown table include wire tokens as a separate column?
Inspect tokensquash/__init__.py and ensure the public API exports the useful functions only.
Add support for reading plain text prompt files separated by blank lines; test it.
Run all local checks and push the branch if everything passes.
Please do a code review of this repo and lead with bugs, not a summary.
Find where the CLI joins positional arguments and explain any quoting caveats on PowerShell.
Add docs for why base64 and binary-looking encodings can cost more tokens than plain compact text.
